title: Project Backlog Schema
description: Authoritative schema for BACKLOG.json — the project-level coordination file consumed by /base:orient, /base:next, base:project-curator, /base:feature, and /base:bug.

version.description: Backlog grammar version. v3 is JSON; v1/v2 were markdown.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last mutation. Set by every write script.

epics.description: One entry per specs/epic-*/ directory. Seeded by /base:backlog init, maintained by /base:feature Step 3 and Step 7, mutated by base:project-curator's update_epics_section action.

findings.description: Open working set. Soft cap ~15 entries — beyond that /base:orient nags to prune.

archive.description: Rejected findings only. Append-only. Never pruned.

path.description: Path to the epic directory, trailing slash.

status.description: Mapped from epic-state.json#status: planning|in_progress→IN_PROGRESS, done→DONE, escalated→ESCALATED. UNKNOWN when state file is missing or malformed.

next_action.description: Short hint for a returning human or agent (e.g. 'resume on story 3', 'awaiting decider verdict', 'ready to ship').

slug.description: Kebab-case identifier coined at write time from the finding's text. The base slug is capped at 50 chars; a collision-disambiguator suffix (`-2` ... `-99`) may extend the total to 53 chars. Stable for the finding's lifetime; unique across findings[].

scope.description: Which project the finding belongs to. Common values: 'any', 'base-plugin', or a plugin slug (e.g. 'nostr-skills') or a consumer-project basename. Inferred at write time from the anchor.

anchor.description: Where the finding points in the codebase. null for cross-cutting findings with no specific file location.

text.description: One line, present-tense, specific. The full finding prose.

deferred.description: Present iff the finding was stamped by an auto-dispatched worker that decided not to proceed. Stamped bullets are skipped by /base:next document-order walks and excluded from workload-pressure scanners.

line.description: Single line number. Mutually exclusive with range.

range.description: Line range [start, end]. Mutually exclusive with line.

reason.description: Closed enum. Adding a new value is a schema change. See plugins/base/skills/backlog/references/format.md for semantics and paths to resolution.

detail.description: Short description of why the stamp landed. Worker's abort-reason text, line-precise hunk evidence, gate/escalation reason, or original bullet text for legacy-orphan.

text.description: Verbatim text of the rejected finding.

reason.description: Why it was rejected. Cite evidence: a test, a decider verdict, a file.

source_slug.description: Optional: the slug of the finding that became this archive entry. Helps audit trails link rejection back to the original finding when slug records pre-date the rejection.

adr.description: Optional: ADR identifier when the archive cluster was promoted via base:project-curator's promote_rejections_to_adr action. Marks the entry as adopted into a durable architectural record without deleting it from the archive.
